zone_6b_notes: Missouri native shrub â€” one of the most ecologically valuable shrubs for Zone 6b landscapes. White flat-topped flower clusters in Mayâ€“June are visited by native bees; blue-black berries in Septemberâ€“October feed dozens of bird species (cedar waxwings, thrushes, bluebirds). Brilliant orange-red fall foliage. Extremely adaptable â€” tolerates full sun to partial shade, moist to dry soils, wet edges. Blue Muffin is a compact cultivar (5 Ã— 5 ft); Chicago Lustre is the standard large form (8 Ã— 8 ft). Plant two different clones for best berry production (cross-pollination). Essentially pest-free in Missouri when sited correctly.

planting.best_planting_time: Plant nursery stock in spring or fall
